This is a good basic structure to use early in the project when productivity is important. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | Remember, as your software grows, your structure must grow too. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | # **Modules** | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## Core | MCP:6vSAY9 | Each submodule of this module is responsible for one domain service. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | This must make the modular structure grow with the growth of the service. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ### core:core-api | MCP:6vSAY9 | It is the only executable module in the project. It is structured to have domains to maximize initial development productivity. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | It is also responsible for providing APIs and setting up frameworks for services. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ### core:core-enum | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | This module contains enums that are used by `core-api` and must be delivered to external modules. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 |
| MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## Clients | MCP:6vSAY9 | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with external systems. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ### clients:clients-example | MCP:6vSAY9 | This module shows an example of HTTP communication with `Spring-Cloud-Open-Feign`. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 |
| MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## Storage | MCP:6vSAY9 | Submodules of this module are responsible for integrating with the various storages. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ### storage:db-core | MCP:6vSAY9 | This module shows an example of connecting to `MySql` using `Spring-Data-JPA`. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 |
| MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## Support | MCP:6vSAY9 | Submodules of this module are responsible for additional support. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ### support:logging | MCP:6vSAY9 | This module supports logging of service and has a dependency added for distributed tracing support. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | It also includes dependencies to support `Sentry`. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ### support:monitoring | MCP:6vSAY9 | This module supports monitoring of services. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 |
| MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## Tests | MCP:6vSAY9 | Submodules of this module are responsible for the convenience of writing test codes. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ### tests:api-docs | MCP:6vSAY9 | This module is for writing spring-rest-docs conveniently. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 |
| MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | # Dependency Management | MCP:6vSAY9 | All dependency versioning is done through `gradle.properties` file. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | If you want to add a new dependency, put the version in `gradle.properties` and load it in `build.gradle`. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 |
| MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | # Runtime Profiles | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## local | MCP:6vSAY9 | This profile aims to configure an environment that can be developed even if the network is disconnected. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## local-dev | MCP:6vSAY9 | This profile aims configurations that allow me to connect to the DEV environment from my local machine. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## dev | MCP:6vSAY9 | This profile exists for deploying Development environments. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## staging | MCP:6vSAY9 | This profile exists for deploying Staging environments. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## live | MCP:6vSAY9 | This profile exists for deploying Live environments. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 |
| MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | # Test Tasks & Tags | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## test | MCP:6vSAY9 | This is a collection of test-tasks that we want to run on `CI`. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | If you want to change the settings, modify the `build.gradle` file. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## unitTest | MCP:6vSAY9 | This is a group of tests that typically have no dependencies, are fast to run, and test a single feature. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## contextTest | MCP:6vSAY9 | This is a task that runs with SpringContext and has integration tests. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## restDocsTest | MCP:6vSAY9 | This is a task to create asciidoc based on spring-rest-docs. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## developTest | MCP:6vSAY9 | This is a task of tests that should not be run in `CI`. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | This is a good tag to use if you're not good at writing tests. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 |
| MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | # Recommended Preferences | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## Git Hook | MCP:6vSAY9 | This setting makes run `lint` on every commit. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ``` | MCP:6vSAY9 | $ git config core.hookspath .githooks | MCP:6vSAY9 | ``` | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ## IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:6vSAY9 | This setting makes it easier to run the `test code` out of the box. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | ``` | MCP:6vSAY9 | // Gradle Build and run with IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:6vSAY9 | Build, Execution, Deployment > Build Tools > Gradle > Run tests using > IntelliJ IDEA | MCP:6vSAY9 | ``` | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | If you want to apply lint settings to the format of IDEA, please refer to the guide below. | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | [Spring Java Format IntelliJ IDEA](https://github.com/spring-io/spring-javaformat#intellij-idea) | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | --- | MCP:6vSAY9 | | MCP:6vSAY9 | # Supported By | MCP:6vSAY9 |

Here is a summary of the module structure of this Spring Boot Java Template:

---

## Module Structure Overview

This is a **multi-module Gradle project** (`spring-boot-java-template`) organized into **5 major module groups**, each containing one or more submodules. The design philosophy is to start with a simple, productive structure that can grow with the project.

```
spring-boot-java-template (root)
├── core/
│   ├── core-api        ← The main executable Spring Boot application
│   └── core-enum       ← Shared enums
├── clients/
│   └── client-example  ← External HTTP client integration (OpenFeign)
├── storage/
│   └── db-core         ← Database / JPA persistence layer
├── support/
│   ├── logging         ← Logging, distributed tracing, Sentry
│   └── monitoring      ← Actuator & Prometheus metrics
└── tests/
    └── api-docs        ← Spring REST Docs test helpers
```

---

### 1. **`core`** — Business Domain & Application Entry Point

| Submodule | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| **`core-api`** | The **only executable module** (produces a `bootJar`). Contains the Spring Boot application class (`CoreApiApplication`), REST controllers (versioned under `controller/v1/`), domain services, request/response DTOs, global exception handling (`ApiControllerAdvice`, `CoreException`, `ErrorType`), and a generic `ApiResponse` wrapper. It depends on **all other modules** (`core-enum`, `storage:db-core`, `clients:client-example`, `support:logging`, `support:monitoring`), acting as the assembly point for the entire application. |
| **`core-enum`** | A lightweight library of **shared enumerations** (e.g. `ExampleEnum`) used by `core-api` and any other module that needs them. Has no external dependencies. |

**Intent:** Each submodule under `core` is meant to represent a domain service. As the application grows, new domain-specific submodules (e.g. `core-user`, `core-order`) would be added here alongside `core-api`.

---

### 2. **`clients`** — External System Integrations

| Submodule | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| **`client-example`** | Demonstrates how to integrate with **external HTTP APIs** using **Spring Cloud OpenFeign**. Contains a Feign client interface (`ExampleApi`), a wrapper client class (`ExampleClient`), request/response DTOs, and configuration (`ExampleConfig`). Ships with its own YAML config (`client-example.yml`) and test resources. |

**Intent:** Every new external service integration (REST APIs, third-party services) gets its own submodule under `clients/`, isolating the client code and its dependencies from the rest of the app.

---

### 3. **`storage`** — Data Persistence

| Submodule | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| **`db-core`** | Shows how to connect to **MySQL (and H2 for tests)** using **Spring Data JPA**. Contains a `BaseEntity` (auditing base class), an `ExampleEntity`, an `ExampleRepository`, and JPA/datasource configuration (`CoreDataSourceConfig`, `CoreJpaConfig`). The multi-profile YAML (`db-core.yml`) configures datasource settings for each environment (local, dev, staging, live). |

**Intent:** Each storage concern (e.g. a primary DB, a read replica, a Redis cache) gets its own submodule under `storage/`, keeping persistence code cleanly separated from business logic.

---

### 4. **`support`** — Cross-Cutting Infrastructure Concerns

| Submodule | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| **`logging`** | Provides **logback configuration files** for each runtime profile (local, local-dev, dev, staging, live). Pulls in `spring-boot-starter-opentelemetry` for **distributed tracing** and `sentry-logback` for **error reporting to Sentry**. |
| **`monitoring`** | Provides **Spring Boot Actuator** endpoints and **Micrometer → Prometheus** metrics export, configured via `monitoring.yml`. Enables health checks, metrics, and observability out of the box. |

**Intent:** Non-functional, operational concerns (logging, tracing, metrics, alerting) live in `support/` so they can be added or removed from the executable without touching business code.

---

### 5. **`tests`** — Test Utilities & Documentation

| Submodule | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|
| **`api-docs`** | A test-scoped library providing `RestDocsTest` — a base test class that sets up **Spring REST Docs** with MockMvc. Makes it easy to write API documentation tests (generates AsciiDoc snippets) in `core-api` or any other module. |

**Intent:** Shared test helpers, fixtures, and documentation generators go under `tests/` so they can be pulled in as `testImplementation` dependencies without polluting production classpaths.

---

## Dependency Graph (simplified)

```
core-api (bootJar)
 ├── core-enum
 ├── storage:db-core  ──→ core-enum
 ├── clients:client-example
 ├── support:logging
 ├── support:monitoring
 └── (test) tests:api-docs
```

The root `build.gradle` applies **Spring Dependency Management**, **Spring Cloud BOM**, and common test dependencies (JUnit 5, AssertJ, Mockito) to all subprojects, ensuring consistent versions project-wide. All version numbers are centralized in `gradle.properties`.
